canonical_definition: Flores-101 is consistently characterized across the provided literature as a benchmark dataset for evaluating multilingual capabilities, with a primary focus on machine translation. The dataset is described as containing professionally or human-written sentences with "aligned translations across 101 languages" ("VTechAGP: An Academic-to-General-Audience Text Paraphrase Dataset and Benchmark Models"). Its most prevalent application is to measure the performance of machine translation, with evidence showing its use for evaluating bidirectional translation tasks. A smaller body of work frames its use more broadly for assessing "multilingual general and cross-lingual generation capabilities" ("LyapLock: Bounded Knowledge Preservation in Sequential Large Language Model Editing"). The methods for evaluation on this benchmark are described in slightly different ways; some sources mention its use for qualitative evaluation, while another specifies the use of quantitative metrics like BLEU and COMET to assess translation quality. Overall, Flores-101 is operationalized as a common instrument for testing model performance on translation across a large number of languages.
papers: Leveraging Text-to-Text Transformers as Classifier Chain for Few-Shot Multi-Label Classification
LyapLock: Bounded Knowledge Preservation in Sequential Large Language Model Editing
CRScore: Grounding Automated Evaluation of Code Review Comments in Code Claims and Smells
VTechAGP: An Academic-to-General-Audience Text Paraphrase Dataset and Benchmark Models

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset consisting of sentences professionally translated across 101 languages, used for qualitative evaluation of interventions in a machine translation context.
definitions.paper: CRScore: Grounding Automated Evaluation of Code Review Comments in Code Claims and Smells

definitions.text: Benchmark for evaluating machine translation and cross-lingual generation across 101 languages, using BLEU and COMET metrics to assess translation quality.
definitions.paper: LyapLock: Bounded Knowledge Preservation in Sequential Large Language Model Editing

relationships.paper: MindMerger: Efficiently Boosting LLM Reasoning in non-English Languages
relationships.evidence: We perform the same interventions on the Flores-101 dataset (Goyal et al., 2022), which provides aligned translations across 101 languages. We present a selection of qualitative examples in Figure 7. (Section 4.3)
